# Netflix Data Concept 
# Databse used: Snowflake

This analytics model uses entities to represent physical tables with raw data, 
while metrics not actual columsn but are reusable calculations that make the data analysis-ready. 
Relationships are the defined logical rules that join these tables together. 

metrics:
  # --- Numeric Metrics ---
  - metric_name: "Invoice Amount (USD)"
    aliases: ["Revenue in Dollars", "USD Payments"]
    description: "The numeric value of an invoice ONLY IF it is in USD. Returns NULL for other currencies. Use this metric directly in a SUM() aggregation to get the total dollar revenue. To use this in SQL, you MUST use its 'calculation_logic' expression. This is NOT a physical column."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    source_column: "total"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN total LIKE '$%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(total, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END"
    data_type: "DECIMAL(10,2)"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "What is the total revenue in dollars?"
        sql_snippet: "SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN i.total LIKE '$%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(i.total, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END) FROM invoice i"
  - metric_name: "Invoice Amount (EUR)"
    description: "The numeric value of an invoice ONLY IF it is in EUR. Returns NULL for other currencies. Use this metric directly in a SUM() aggregation to get the total euro revenue. To use this in SQL, you MUST use its 'calculation_logic' expression. This is NOT a physical column."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    source_column: "total"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN total LIKE '€%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(total, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END"
    data_type: "DECIMAL(10,2)"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "Calculate the sum of all payments in euros."
        sql_snippet: "SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN i.total LIKE '€%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(i.total, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END) FROM invoice i"
  - metric_name: "Invoice Currency"
    description: "The original CURRENCY CODE ('USD', 'EUR') of the invoice. Use this for filtering or grouping by currency type. To use this in SQL, you MUST use its 'calculation_logic' expression."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    source_column: "total"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN total LIKE '$%' THEN 'USD' WHEN total LIKE '€%' THEN 'EUR' ELSE 'UNKNOWN' END"
    data_type: "VARCHAR(7)"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "Show the number of invoices per currency."
        sql_snippet: "SELECT (CASE WHEN i.total LIKE '$%' THEN 'USD' WHEN i.total LIKE '€%' THEN 'EUR' ELSE 'UNKNOWN' END), COUNT(*) FROM invoice i GROUP BY 1"
  - metric_name: "Cost Per Stream (USD)"
    description: "The numeric value of the content cost ONLY IF it is in USD. Returns NULL otherwise. Use this for summing up costs in dollars. To use this in SQL, you MUST use its 'calculation_logic' expression."
    applies_to_entity: "content"
    source_column: "cost_per_stream"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN cost_per_stream LIKE '$%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(cost_per_stream, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END"
    data_type: "DECIMAL(10,2)"
  - metric_name: "Cost Per Stream (EUR)"
    description: "The numeric value of the content cost ONLY IF it is in EUR. Returns NULL otherwise. Use this for summing up costs in euros. To use this in SQL, you MUST use its 'calculation_logic' expression."
    applies_to_entity: "content"
    source_column: "cost_per_stream"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN cost_per_stream LIKE '€%' THEN CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(cost_per_stream, '[^0-9.]', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2)) ELSE NULL END"
    data_type: "DECIMAL(10,2)"
  - metric_name: "Cost Currency"
    description: "The original CURRENCY CODE ('USD', 'EUR') of the content cost. Use this for filtering or grouping by currency type."
    applies_to_entity: "content"
    source_column: "cost_per_stream"
    calculation_logic: "CASE WHEN cost_per_stream LIKE '$%' THEN 'USD' WHEN cost_per_stream LIKE '€%' THEN 'EUR' ELSE 'UNKNOWN' END"
    data_type: "VARCHAR(7)"

  # --- Date/Time Metrics ---
  - metric_name: "Typed Service Start Date"
    description: "The proper DATE type for the start of an invoice service period. Use this for all date-based filtering and operations on the service start."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    source_column: "service_period"
    calculation_logic: "TO_DATE(SPLIT_PART(service_period, ' to ', 1), 'YYYY-MM-DD')"
    data_type: "DATE"
  - metric_name: "Typed Service End Date"
    description: "The proper DATE type for the end of an invoice service period. Use this for all date-based filtering and operations on the service end."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    source_column: "service_period"
    calculation_logic: "TO_DATE

entities.name: INVOICE
entities.description: A table containing invoice data, including details such as total amount, service period, and currency information. It serves as the primary entity for financial transaction records}

entities.name: CONTENT
entities.description: A table storing content-related information, including cost per stream data and associated currency details for Netflix media assets}

entities.name: SNOWFLAKE
entities.description: The cloud-based data platform used to store and process the analytical data models and metrics}

entities.name: TOTAL
entities.description: A source column in the invoice table containing raw amount values with currency symbols (e.g., '$15.99'), requiring processing for numerical analysis}

entities.name: COST_PER_STREAM
entities.description: A source column in the content table storing raw cost values with currency symbols (e.g., '€0.05'), used for content expenditure calculations}

entities.name: SERVICE_PERIOD
entities.description: A source column in the invoice table containing date range strings (e.g., '2023-01-01 to 2023-01-31'), representing active service durations}

entities.name: INVOICE AMOUNT (USD)
entities.description: Calculated metric extracting USD values from total column using regex, returning NULL for non-USD entries. Used in SUM() aggregations for revenue analysis}

entities.name: INVOICE AMOUNT (EUR)
entities.description: Calculated metric extracting EUR values from total column using regex, returning NULL for non-EUR entries. Supports euro-denominated revenue reporting}

entities.name: INVOICE CURRENCY
entities.description: Derived metric identifying currency codes ('USD','EUR') based on symbol presence in total column. Enables currency-specific filtering and grouping}

entities.name: COST PER STREAM (USD)
entities.description: Calculated metric extracting USD cost values from cost_per_stream column using regex. Essential for content cost analysis in dollars}

entities.name: COST PER STREAM (EUR)
entities.description: Calculated metric extracting EUR cost values from cost_per_stream column using regex. Supports content cost analysis in euros}

entities.name: COST CURRENCY
entities.description: Derived metric identifying currency codes ('USD','EUR') from cost_per_stream column symbols. Facilitates currency-based cost categorization}

entities.name: TYPED SERVICE START DATE
entities.description: Date-conversion metric parsing the first part of service_period strings into DATE type for temporal operations and filtering}

entities.name: TYPED SERVICE END DATE
entities.description: Date-conversion metric parsing the second part of service_period strings into DATE type for period-end analysis}

entities.name: ENTITIES
entities.description: Physical tables containing raw data that serve as foundational elements in the analytics model}

entities.name: METRICS
entities.description: Reusable calculations applied to entities that transform raw data into analysis-ready quantitative measures}

entities.name: RELATIONSHIPS
entities.description: Defined logical rules joining tables together to enable integrated data analysis across entities}

entities.name: NUMERIC METRICS
entities.description: Metrics handling quantitative values, specifically currency amounts and numerical conversions}

entities.name: DATE/TIME METRICS
entities.description: Metrics focused on temporal data processing and date-type conversions}

entities.name: ALIASES
entities.description: Alternative names for metrics that facilitate natural language querying and user recognition}

entities.name: DESCRIPTION
entities.description: Detailed explanations clarifying metric purpose, behavior, and usage constraints}

entities.name: CALCULATION_LOGIC
entities.description: SQL expressions defining how metrics derive values from source columns through transformations}

entities.name: DATA_TYPE
entities.description: Defines the storage format and validation rules for metric output values}

entities.name: NLQ_EXAMPLES
entities.description: Demonstrations linking natural language questions to SQL implementations using specific metrics}

entities.name: NATURAL_LANGUAGE_QUERY
entities.description: User-friendly question formulations that map to underlying metric calculations}

entities.name: SQL_SNIPPET
entities.description: Executable code examples showing practical implementation of metrics in analytical queries}

    source_column: "service_period"

    data_type: "DATE"

    description: "The 'stream_date' column converted to a proper DATE type. Use this for all date-based operations."
    applies_to_entity: "streams"
    source_column: "stream_date"
    format_string: "DD-Mon-YYYY"
    calculation_logic: "TO_DATE(stream_date)"
    data_type: "DATE"
  - metric_name: "Year of Stream"
    description: "The year component of the stream date. Useful for annual reporting."
    applies_to_entity: "streams"
    source_column: "stream_date"
    calculation_logic: "EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(stream_date))"
    data_type: "INT"
  - metric_name: "Month of Stream"
    description: "The month number (1-12) of the stream date. Useful for monthly reporting."
    applies_to_entity: "streams"
    source_column: "stream_date"
    calculation_logic: "EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_DATE(stream_date))"
    data_type: "INT"
  - metric_name: "Typed Invoice Date"

relationships:
  - name: "streams_to_customer"
    semantic_description: "Links a customer's individual streaming events to their main account profile."
    from_entity: "streams"
    to_entity: "customer"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - { from_column: "customer_id", to_column: "customer_id" }
  - name: "streams_to_content"
    semantic_description: "Connects a specific streaming event to the content that was watched."
    from_entity: "streams"
    to_entity: "content"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - { from_column: "content_id", to_column: "content_id" }
  - name: "invoices_to_customer"
    semantic_description: "Associates a customer's billing records with their main account profile."
    usage_hint: "Use this join for any query combining billing information (plan, total) with customer attributes (state, country)."
    from_entity: "invoice"
    to_entity: "customer"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - { from_column: "customer_id", to_column: "customer_id" }
  - name: "invoice_to_streams"
    semantic_description: "A multi-hop relationship connecting billing to streaming activity via the customer. Essential for analyzing viewing behavior of customers on specific plans (e.g., 'What do Premium users watch?')."
    from_entity: "invoice"
    to_entity: "streams"
    cardinality: "ONE_TO_MANY"
    join_path:
      - { from: "invoice", to: "customer", on: "customer_id" }
      - { from: "customer", to: "streams", on: "customer_id" }

entities.name: CUSTOMER
entities.description: Represents a customer with attributes like last name, email, phone, location, and account details. Attributes include personal identification and sign-up information.

entities.name: CONTENT
entities.description:
Represents media titles in a catalog, including descriptive metadata, financial metrics, and distribution information.>
Represents media titles in a catalog, including attributes like ID, title, genre, duration, cost metrics, release date, and distributor information.

entities.name: LAST_NAME
entities.description:
The family name of the customer, used for identification and personalization.
The family name identifier for a customer, used in personalization and identification.>

entities.name: EMAIL_ID
entities.description:
Unique email address associated with the customer's account, used for communication.
Unique email address associated with a customer account, serving as a communication channel.>

entities.name: PHONE_NUMBER
entities.description:
Primary contact phone number for the customer.
Primary contact number for customer communication and support.>

entities.name: STATE
entities.description:
The state or province where the customer resides, indicating geographical location.
Geographical region where a customer resides, used for demographic analysis.>

entities.name: COUNTRY
entities.description:
National location of a customer, used for regional segmentation.>
The country where the customer resides, indicating geographical location.

entities.name: SIGN_UP_DATE
entities.description:
Account creation date stored as text; requires derived metrics for date operations.
Raw text field recording account creation date; requires conversion for operational use.>

entities.name: CONTENT_ID
entities.description:
Unique identifier for each media content item, serving as a primary key.
Unique numeric identifier for media items, serving as primary key.>

entities.name: TITLE
entities.description:
Official display name of the content item, such as a movie or show title.
Official display name of media content for identification and search.>

entities.name: EPISODE
entities.description:
Episode number for series content (0 for movies), indicating sequence or type.
Sequence indicator for episodic content (0 denotes non-episodic media).>

entities.name: GENRE
entities.description:
Primary classification of content, potentially as a pipe-separated list of categories.
Classification category/categories describing media content type.>

entities.name: DURATION
entities.description:
Total runtime of the content in minutes.
Total runtime length in minutes for media consumption.>

entities.name: COST_PER_STREAM
entities.description:
Raw text field containing licensed view cost with currency symbol; requires derivation for calculations.>
Raw licensing cost per view (with currency), requiring derived metrics for calculations.

entities.name: RELEASE_DATE
entities.description:
Original release date stored as text; requires conversion for date operations.>
Original release date stored as text; requires derived metrics for date operations.

entities.name: DISTRIBUTOR
entities.description:
Entity holding distribution rights for media content.>
Rights holder or distributor responsible for the content.

entities.name: TYPED_SIGN_UP_DATE
entities.description: Derived date metric enabling temporal operations on customer sign-up events.>

entities.name: COST_PER_STREAM_USD
entities.description: Derived metric representing licensing cost in US dollars for financial analysis.>

entities.name: COST_PER_STREAM_EUR
entities.description: Derived metric representing licensing cost in Euros for financial analysis.>

entities.name: COST_CURRENCY
entities.description: Derived metric identifying currency type from raw cost data.>

entities.name: TYPED_RELEASE_DATE
entities.description: Derived date metric enabling temporal operations on content release events.>
